2605.10146.title: Benchmarking Safety Risks of Knowledge-Intensive Reasoning under Malicious Knowledge Editing
2605.10146.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly rely on knowledge editing to support knowledge-intensive reasoning, but this flexibility also introduces critical safety risks: adversaries can inject malicious or misleading knowledge that corrupts downstream reasoning and leads to harmful outcomes. Existing knowledge editing benchmarks primarily focus on editing efficacy and lack a unified framework for systematically evaluating the safety implications of edited knowledge on reasoning behavior. To address this gap, we present EditRisk-Bench, a benchmark for systematically evaluating safety risks of knowledge-intensive reasoning under malicious knowledge editing. Unlike prior benchmarks that mainly emphasize edit success, generalization, and locality, EditRisk-Bench focuses on how injected knowledge affects downstream reasoning behavior and reliability. It integrates diverse malicious scenarios, including misinformation, bias, and safety violations, together with multi-level knowledge-intensive reasoning tasks and representative editing strategies within a unified evaluation framework measuring attack effectiveness, reasoning correctness, and side effects. Extensive experiments on both open-source and closed-source LLMs show that malicious knowledge editing can reliably induce incorrect or unsafe reasoning while largely preserving general capabilities, making such risks difficult to detect. We further identify several key factors influencing these risks, including edit scale, knowledge characteristics, and reasoning complexity. EditRisk-Bench provides an extensible testbed for understanding and mitigating safety risks in knowledge editing for LLMs.
2605.10146.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越依賴知識編輯來支持知識密集型推理，但這種靈活性也引入了關鍵的安全風險：對手可以注入惡意或誤導性的知識，從而破壞下游推理並導致有害結果。現有的知識編輯基準主要集中在編輯效率上，缺乏一個統一的框架來系統性評估編輯知識對推理行為的安全影響。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了EditRisk-Bench，這是一個系統性評估在惡意知識編輯下知識密集型推理的安全風險的基準。與之前主要強調編輯成功、泛化和局部性的基準不同，EditRisk-Bench專注於注入的知識如何影響下游推理行為和可靠性。它整合了多種惡意場景，包括錯誤信息、偏見和安全違規，並結合多層次的知識密集型推理任務和具有代表性的編輯策略，形成一個統一的評估框架，測量攻擊效果、推理正確性和副作用。在開源和閉源的LLMs上進行的廣泛實驗表明，惡意知識編輯可以可靠地引起不正確或不安全的推理，同時在很大程度上保持一般能力，使這些風險難以檢測。我們進一步確定了幾個影響這些風險的關鍵因素，包括編輯規模、知識特徵和推理複雜性。EditRisk-Bench提供了一個可擴展的測試平台，用於理解和減輕LLMs知識編輯中的安全風險。
2605.10146.author: Qinghua Mao et.al.
2605.10146.authors: Qinghua Mao, Xi Lin, Jinze Gu, Jun Wu, Siyuan Li, Yuliang Chen
